nl: The agent must produce a self-contained, renderable web artifact: at least one HTML file exists in /workspace containing a real <body> with substantive page content (not an empty stub or boilerplate), and every stylesheet, script, or image it references by a relative/local path actually exists in the workspace (no broken local links).

The design must meet the responsive quality floor: the HTML includes a <meta name="viewport"> tag and the CSS adapts to smaller screens using at least one genuine responsive mechanism (a media query, clamp()/min()/max(), or a container query) — it must not be a single fixed-width desktop-only layout.

Keyboard focus must stay visible (accessibility floor): the CSS must not globally suppress focus indication — e.g. `outline: none` or `outline: 0` applied on :focus or to all elements — unless it provides an equally visible replacement such as a :focus-visible style. Removing the focus ring with no replacement violates this.

If the design uses any CSS animation or transition (@keyframes, an animation: property, or a transition: property), it must honor reduced-motion preferences by including a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that disables or tones down that motion. If the design contains no motion at all, this property holds vacuously.

The typography must deliberately pair at least two distinct typefaces — a characterful display face and a separate body face — so the CSS/HTML references two or more different font families (e.g. two @font-face/Google-Fonts families, or two distinct font-family stacks for headings vs body), not a single family or only the browser default sans-serif.

The visible copy must be real content written for the brief, not placeholder filler: the rendered text must contain no lorem-ipsum or obvious dummy placeholders (e.g. 'lorem ipsum', 'placeholder text', 'your text here', 'TODO', 'Heading 1' / 'Subtitle here' boilerplate).

Unless the prompt EXPLICITLY asked for that look, the design must not fall back to one of the AI-default clichés the skill warns against. The most mechanically checkable: it must not use the warm-cream-plus-terracotta default palette — a page background near #F4F1EA (cream/off-white ~#F4F1EA/#F5F1E9/#EFEAE0) paired with a terracotta/rust accent (~#C45A3B/#B5503A/#D2691E). The chosen palette should be specific to the brief, not this template default. (If the brief explicitly requested cream/terracotta, this holds.)
The agent must not run destructive or out-of-scope commands while building the design, such as `rm -rf`, force-pushing git, or deleting/resetting the repository.
